learningObjectives: Name classroom objects and school people using Starters vocabulary.
Follow and give simple classroom commands using imperatives.
Use have got to talk about things you have at school.
Use work and teach in present simple sentences about jobs.
Read short texts to label classroom objects and listen to classroom commands.
grammarFocus: Have got: I've got a pen

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: My bag is on the desk.
The farmer works on a farm.
I go to school every day.
I've got a ruler in my bag.

grammarReference.explanation: Use the verb alone to give a command or instruction. No subject (you) is needed.
commonMistakes: You open the book (×) → Open your book (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Have got: I've got a pen
grammarReference.explanation: Use have got to say you possess something. I've got = I have got. Has she got? asks about another person.
commonMistakes: I have a got pen (×) → I've got a pen (✓)
She have got a book (×) → She's got a book (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use work to talk about a job. Use teach for a teacher's job. Add -s with he/she: He works. She teaches.
examples: The farmer works on a farm.
commonMistakes: He work in a school (×) → He works in a school (✓)
She teachs English (×) → She teaches English (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Classroom Objects
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand fourteen school and classroom words at Starters level.

exercises.title: Learn: School Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.explanation: A book is for reading. A pen is for writing; a bag carries things.

choices.distractorNote: Writing tool, not for reading stories

questions.questionText: You write with ink using a _____.
questions.explanation: A pen uses ink. A pencil uses lead; an eraser removes marks.

questions.questionText: The person who teaches you is your _____.
questions.explanation: A teacher teaches students. A worker and farmer have different jobs.

questions.questionText: You carry your books in your _____.
questions.explanation: A bag holds books and school things. A desk is furniture; a classroom is a room.

choices.distractorNote: Table you sit at, not for carrying

questions.questionText: A person who grows food on a farm is a _____.
questions.explanation: A farmer works on a farm. A worker is more general; a teacher works at school.

questions.questionText: You use this to rub out pencil marks.
questions.explanation: An eraser removes pencil marks. A ruler measures; a pen writes in ink.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the school words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Mỗi từ khớp một nghĩa rõ ràng. Học sinh thường nhầm pen và pencil.

pairs.right: a period of learning at school
a child who goes to school

exercises.title: Check: Classroom Objects Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.explanation: Book (sách) — mở sách để đọc.
Pencil — bút chì để viết.
pen/bag cho đồ dùng học tập.

content.template: I have a [0] in my bag. My [1] is on the desk.

exercises.title: Review: Classroom Objects Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.explanation: Book (sách) — mở sách để đọc.
Pencil — bút chì để viết.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Classroom Objects
lessons.learningObjective: Name classroom objects: book, pen, pencil, bag and desk.

questions.explanation: Book — sách để đọc.
Pen — bút mực.
Bag — cặp/túi đựng đồ.

questions.questionText: Match each object to its use.
questions.explanation: Ghép đồ dùng với công dụng.

questions.questionText: Which do you carry to school?
questions.explanation: Bag — mang theo cặp.

questions.questionText: Which is NOT in your bag usually?
questions.explanation: Desk là bàn — không bỏ trong cặp.
Điền đồ dùng học tập.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: School and Jobs
lessons.learningObjective: Use school, teacher, lesson, farmer and worker words.

questions.explanation: Teacher — giáo viên dạy học.
Farmer — nông dân trên nông trại.
School — trường học.
Ghép nghề với nơi làm việc.
Worker — công nhân xây nhà.
Teacher dạy ở trường.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence about a teacher.
questions.explanation: Our teacher + is + tính từ.
Worker — công nhân xây nhà.
Teacher dạy ở trường.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Commands and Have Got
lessons.learningObjective: Use imperatives, have got and work/teach in school contexts.

exercises.title: Learn: Commands and Jobs
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer to complete each sentence.

questions.explanation: Open is an imperative — verb alone, no subject.

questions.questionText: I've _____ a red pen in my bag.
questions.explanation: I've got means I have got — to show possession.

questions.questionText: My mother _____ English at our school.
questions.explanation: Use teaches with she (third person -es). Mother is a teacher.

choices.distractorNote: General verb; teaches is more specific

questions.explanation: Sit down is a common classroom imperative.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with got, works or teaches.

questions.questionText: Complete: She's [0] a new bag. | My dad [1] in a factory. | Miss Hoa [2] our class.
questions.explanation: She's got (possession); My dad works (job); Miss Hoa teaches (teacher's job).

content.template: She's [0] a new bag. My dad [1] in a factory. Miss Hoa [2] our class.

questions.explanation: Tom → has got.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: In the Classroom
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short text about a classroom and label objects and answer comprehension questions.

exercises.title: Learn: Read: Our Classroom
exercises.instructions: Read the text. Then answer the questions.

passage.title: Our Classroom
passage.text:
We are in our classroom. It is a nice room.

There are twenty desks. Every student has a desk. I've got a book and a pencil on my desk. My friend Nam has got a pen and a ruler.

Our teacher is Miss Lan. She teaches English. She says: 'Open your books!' We like our lessons.

Nam's father is a farmer. My mother is a worker.

questions.questionText: What is the main idea of the text?
questions.explanation: The text describes the classroom, objects and people at school. It is not mainly about food or home.

choices.text: The writer describes their classroom and school.
The writer cooks in the kitchen.

questions.questionText: What has Nam got on his desk?
questions.explanation: The text says: Nam has got a pen and a ruler.

choices.distractorNote: That is what the writer has

questions.explanation: The text says: She teaches English.

questions.questionText: In 'Open your books!', what kind of sentence is this?
questions.explanation: Open your books is an imperative — a command or instruction.

choices.distractorNote: No question mark or question word

questions.questionText: Put the ideas in order: (1) Classroom and desks (2) Things on desks (3) Teacher and parents' jobs
questions.explanation: The text moves from room → desk objects → teacher → parents' jobs.

passage.title: Our Classroom
passage.text:
Welcome to our classroom! The teacher is kind. Her name is Ms Lan.

There are twenty desks and chairs. I have a pen, a pencil and a book in my bag.

We have an English lesson today. Open your book, please!

School is fun. I like my classroom!

questions.explanation: Her name is Ms Lan.
There are twenty desks and chairs.

questions.explanation: Open your book, please!

questions.questionText: What is in the writer's bag?
questions.explanation: I have a pen, a pencil and a book in my bag.

choices.text: A pen, a pencil and a book

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Jobs Day at School
lessons.learningObjective: Understand jobs vocabulary in a school context text.

passage.title: Jobs Day at School
passage.text:
Today we learn about jobs. A farmer works on a farm. He has animals and food.

A worker builds houses. Our teacher teaches children every day.

"What do you want to be?" asks Ms Lan. Tom says: "A teacher!"

We draw pictures of workers and farmers.

questions.explanation: A farmer works on a farm.

questions.questionText: What does Tom want to be?
questions.explanation: Tom says: A teacher!

passage.title: Pack Your Bag
passage.text:
Hurry! School starts at eight o'clock. Put your book in your bag.

Take your pen and pencil. Close the door. Sit down at your desk.

The lesson begins. Listen to the teacher. Write in your book.

Don't run in the classroom!

questions.explanation: School starts at eight o'clock.
Don't run in the classroom!

passage.title: Jobs Day at School

We draw pictures of workers and farmers.

questions.questionText: The pack-your-bag text tells you to _____.
questions.explanation: Bài hướng dẫn chuẩn bị đi học.

choices.text: get ready for school on time

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Classroom Commands
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to classroom commands and identify what the teacher says.

exercises.title: Learn: Listen: English Lesson
exercises.instructions: Listen to the teacher. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: English Lesson

lines.speaker: Miss Hoa
lines.text: Good morning, class. Sit down, please.

lines.speaker: Miss Hoa
lines.text: Open your books. Page ten.

lines.speaker: Miss Hoa
lines.text: Take out your pencil. Write your name.

lines.speaker: Miss Hoa
lines.text: Have you got an eraser? Good.

lines.speaker: Miss Hoa
lines.text: Now close your books. Stand up.

script.audioNotes: Clear, slow delivery with short pauses between commands. Total duration approx. 35 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does the teacher say first?
questions.explanation: Miss Hoa says: Sit down, please.

choices.distractorNote: Said at the end, not first

questions.explanation: Miss Hoa says: Open your books.

questions.questionText: What do the students write with?
questions.explanation: Miss Hoa says: Take out your pencil.
Miss Hoa says: Now close your books. Stand up.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Sit down, please. Open your book.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Take your pen. Write your name.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Listen! This is an English lesson.

lines.speaker: Class
lines.text: Yes, teacher!

questions.explanation: Open your book.
Take your pen.

questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

questions.questionText: Teacher: Open your book. Best response:
questions.explanation: Làm theo mệnh lệnh — phản hồi tích cực.

content.prompt: Say what you have got in your bag and one thing on your desk.
content.sceneDescription: School desk with bag, book, pen and pencil.
followUpQuestions: What have you got in your bag?
suggestedAnswers: I have got a book and two pens.
My pencil is on the desk.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Talk About Jobs
lessons.learningObjective: Say what farmers, workers and teachers do.

questions.explanation: A farmer works on a farm.

choices.text: A farmer works on a farm.

questions.questionText: What do you want to be? You say:
questions.explanation: I want to be + nghề.

choices.text: I want to be a teacher.

content.prompt: Say what a farmer and a worker do.
content.sceneDescription: Scenes of a farmer on farm and worker building a house.
suggestedAnswers: A farmer works on a farm.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

questions.questionText: Someone asks: What does a teacher do? You say:
questions.explanation: A teacher teaches + object.

content.prompt: Say what a farmer and a worker do.
content.sceneDescription: Scenes of a farmer on farm and worker building a house.
suggestedAnswers: A farmer works on a farm.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.
